text: Every calc_*_dhs() function accepts plain data frame, read data . walkthrough uses dhs_read() parquet archive route AHADI uses internally run_mbg_pipeline() built . route. One DHS files (.dta, .csv, .rds, .sav, …) - read recode sntutils::read() (haven::read_dta()) skip straight Step 2 . analysts take path. mode need dhs_read(), need parquet archive, need directory layout convention. Step 0 (discovery / dictionary inspection) still works - run make_dhs_raw_dictionary(kr) list_dhs_var_labels(kr) data frame just loaded. Archive many surveys (path used ). what: Step 0b: inspect the variables BEFORE building indicators
title: DHS survey analysis
text: step run first. computing indicator, build variable list / data dictionary recode can confirm variables indicator needs present (named expected) survey. make_dhs_raw_dictionary() returns tidy table every variable raw DHS dataset, label - complete “’s file” listing: list_dhs_var_labels() lighter lookup variable labels - handy spot-checking single candidate variable (e.g. survey carry fever item h22, ACT items ml13*?): robust pattern - used example script - build dictionary every recode every survey front save , team can audit variable availability across surveys indicator computed: package also ships curated dictionaries describing indicators (just raw variables) - dhs_dictionary() unified view across domains, per-domain helpers (itn_dictionary(), act_dictionary(), …). See Methodology & conventions.
code: kr <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = "KR",                survey_type = "DHS", country_code = country_iso2,                survey_year = 2017)  # Full variable list for the recode (variable name + label + type) kr_vars <- make_dhs_raw_dictionary(kr) kr_vars #> # variable, label, ... - one row per column in the recode list_dhs_var_labels(kr) recodes <- list(GE = "GE", PR = "PR", HR = "HR", KR = "KR", IR = "IR")  survey_dictionary <- purrr::imap_dfr(recodes, function(ft, nm) {   dat <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = ft,                   survey_type = "DHS", country_code = country_iso2,                   survey_year = 2017)   if (is.null(dat) || nrow(dat) == 0) return(NULL)   make_dhs_raw_dictionary(dplyr::slice(dat, 1:2)) |>     dplyr::mutate(recode = nm, .before = 1) })

title: DHS survey analysis
text: dhs_read() reads single survey recode partitioned parquet archive, preserving value labels survey-specific variables: Recodes map indicator families follows:
code: ge <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = "GE",                survey_type = "DHS", country_code = country_iso2,                survey_year = 2017) pr <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = "PR", ...) hr <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = "HR", ...) kr <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = "KR", ...) ir <- dhs_read(path = path_dhs_parquet, file_type = "IR", ...)

title: DHS survey analysis
text: Every calc_*_dhs() function takes recode(s) needs plus GPS recode (gps_data), admin shapefile, admin_level(s) return: Keep direct survey estimates adm0/adm1. DHS/MIS sample designs stratified region published weights (v005/hv005) representative adm0 adm1 . Going adm2 breaks design assumption - clusters per adm2 sparse, weights longer self-weighting within stratum, direct estimates carry unstable variance wide CIs. sub-region estimates use MBG pipeline instead direct survey aggregation.
code: fever <- calc_fever_dhs(   dhs_kr = kr, gps_data = ge,   shapefile = shp_admin, admin_level = c("adm0", "adm1") )  itn <- calc_itn_dhs(   dhs_hr = hr, dhs_pr = pr, gps_data = ge,   shapefile = shp_admin, admin_level = c("adm0", "adm1") )  pfpr   <- calc_pfpr_dhs(dhs_pr = pr, gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin) csb    <- calc_csb_dhs(dhs_kr = kr, gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin) act    <- calc_act_dhs(dhs_kr = kr, gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin) cm     <- calc_case_management_dhs(dhs_kr = kr, gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin) iptp   <- calc_iptp_dhs(dhs_ir = ir, gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin) wealth <- calc_wealth_dhs(dhs_hr = hr, gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin)  # A few take extra arguments: anemia <- calc_severe_anemia_dhs(dhs_pr = pr, gps_data = ge,                                  shapefile = shp_admin,                                  altitude_adjusted = FALSE) u5mr   <- calc_u5mr_dhs(dhs_kr = kr, period_years = 5,                         gps_data = ge, shapefile = shp_admin)

title: Get started
text: sntmethods R package developed AHADI turns raw survey routine health data sub-national estimates used plan malaria control. analytical companion sntutils: sntutils reads, cleans, harmonises data, sntmethods runs epidemiological methods top . package organised around three workflows, covered article: DHS survey analysis - survey-weighted, design-correct estimates 100+ indicators across 16 domains, straight DHS/MIS microdata. Spatial modeling (MBG) - model-based geostatistics turns cluster-level survey data continuous raster surfaces population-weighted admin estimates. Routine data: incidence & TPR - malaria incidence health-facility data via N0-N5 cascade, test positivity structured fallbacks, trend detection. Two ideas run three: Long-format, admin-stratified outputs. Every estimator returns tidy tibbles keyed admin level (adm0, adm1, adm2) point, ci_l, ci_u, numerator, denominator, indicator metadata - never nested list unpack. Documented methods. indicator family ships machine-readable data dictionary methodology spec inst/methods/. See Methodology & conventions article. New Subnational Tailoring? AHADI SNT Code Library methodology-level companion - country examples analytical reasoning behind every step functions automate.

title: Methodology & conventions
text: Detailed methodology indicator lives machine-readable YAML inst/methods/. file documents DHS variable mappings, inclusion criteria, calculation logic, references / World Malaria Report standards.

title: Methodology & conventions
text: Direct survey estimates stay adm0/adm1. DHS/MIS weights representative region level; sub-region estimates come MBG pipeline, direct aggregation. ACT drug class across multiple variables - artemisinin-based combination therapies count; monotherapies excluded. U5MR reported per 1,000 live births; intervention-coverage indicators percentages (0-100). ITN use_if_access (use among access) computed default standard age groups (u5, 5_14, ov15).

title: Routine data: incidence & TPR
text: calc_tpr() computes TPR per facility-month fills gaps fallback hierarchy (facility rolling average → district → previous year → region → national), flagging fallback used: Audit proxy/fallback logic validate_tpr_proxies():
code: result <- calc_tpr(   dhis2_hf,   hf_var   = "hf_uid",   adm1_var = "adm1",   adm2_var = "adm2",   date_var = "date",   conf_var = "conf",   test_var = "test",   pres_var = "pres",   reporting_threshold = 0.80,   extreme_threshold   = c(0.01, 0.99),   activity_indicators = vars_of_interest,   include_flags = TRUE ) validate_tpr_proxies(result)

title: Routine data: incidence & TPR
text: calc_incidence() runs N-level cascade. level adds adjustment, data must carry columns level needs (tpr_var N1+, reprate_var N2+, care-seeking columns care-seeking adjustment):
code: incidence_input <- result |>   dplyr::left_join(csb_dhs, by = "adm1") |>          # care-seeking shares   dplyr::filter(!is.na(conf), !is.na(test),                 !is.na(pop),  !is.na(pres))  incid <- calc_incidence(   incidence_input,                  # must already contain tpr + reprate   levels   = c("N0", "N1", "N2", "N3"),   hf_var   = "hf_uid",   adm0_var = "adm0", adm1_var = "adm1", adm2_var = "adm2",   date_var = "date", pop_var = "pop",   conf_var = "conf", test_var = "test", pres_var = "pres",   tpr_var     = "tpr",      # required for N1+   reprate_var = "reprate",  # required for N2+   cs_public_var  = "csb_public",   cs_private_var = "csb_private",   cs_none_var    = "csb_no_treatment",   rho = 0,   rate_multiplier = 1000,   # incidence per 1,000 population   include_flags = TRUE )

title: Spatial modeling (MBG)
text: MBG needs three things: cluster table one row per survey cluster columns: cluster_id, indicator (event count / numerator), samplesize (denominator), x (longitude), y (latitude). population raster modeled population (optionally age-specific rasters child / women--reproductive-age denominators). Admin boundaries sf objects (adm0_sf, adm1_sf, adm2_sf).

title: Spatial modeling (MBG)
text: package’s prep_*_mbg() / calc_*_mbg() helpers build cluster table indicator family, just data.table/data.frame - shape, built directly DHS PR recode GE GPS recode (condensed inst/scripts/mbg_pfpr2_10_dhs.R): fit_mbg_indicator() --one wrapper around mbg::MbgModelRunner. Give cluster table, population raster, subset admin shapefiles; fits model returns comprehensive list: result contains: Country/ISO codes derived automatically median cluster coordinate. cache_dir set, prediction matrix cached re-runs skip expensive INLA fit.
code: library(sntmethods)  # cluster-level positives / tested, joined to GPS coordinates pfpr_dt <- data.table::data.table(   cluster_id = pfpr_sf$cluster_id,   indicator  = pfpr_sf$n_positive,  # numerator   samplesize = pfpr_sf$n_tested,    # denominator   x = pfpr_coords[, 1],             # longitude   y = pfpr_coords[, 2]              # latitude ) fit <- fit_mbg_indicator(   cluster_data      = pfpr_dt,   indicator_name    = "pfpr_mic_2_10",   population_raster = pop_rast,   adm1_sf           = adm1_sf,   adm2_sf           = adm2_sf,   primary_level     = "adm2",   output_levels     = c("adm1", "adm2"),   survey_year       = 2016,   source_label      = "DHS",   output_dir        = path_output,            # NULL = write nothing   cache_dir         = fs::path(path_output, "cache")  # caches the INLA fit )

title: Spatial modeling (MBG)
text: mean surface uncertainty plain SpatRasters, mapping tool works. package also provides generate_indicator_map(), generate_all_maps(), cluster diagnostics via plot_mbg_clusters():
code: cell_preds <- fit$cell_predictions r_mean <- cell_preds$mean * 100                          # PfPR as % r_unc  <- (cell_preds$upper - cell_preds$lower) * 100     # 95% CI width (pp)  tmap::tm_shape(r_mean) +   tmap::tm_raster(palette = "YlOrRd", title = "PfPR2-10 (%)") +   tmap::tm_shape(adm2_sf) + tmap::tm_borders(lwd = 0.3)

title: Spatial modeling (MBG)
text: whole country across many indicators, run_mbg_pipeline() orchestrates everything: auto-discovers surveys parquet archive, prepares cluster data per indicator, fits INLA/MBG models, generates prediction rasters, aggregates adm2 (adm3) via population-weighted zonal statistics, rolls adm1 adm0. general-purpose MBG wrapper. run_mbg_pipeline() built around AHADI’s hive-partitioned parquet archive (see ?dhs_read) dhs_read()’s discovery semantics. Use - willing build - archive want fit many indicators across many surveys end--end. ad-hoc, single-survey, single-indicator work, stay fit_mbg_indicator() (Single indicator section ) - takes cluster-level data frame load (e.g. sntutils::read() + prep_*_mbg() helper) runs single MBG fit, archive required. See ?run_mbg_pipeline full use / use checklist. Supported indicator families: pfpr, itn, irs, csb, act, antimalarial, fever, anc, iptp, epi, u5mr, anemia, smc, eff_cm (effective case management).
code: results <- run_mbg_pipeline(   adm0_sf = adm0, adm1_sf = adm1, adm2_sf = adm2,   pop_raster    = list("2016" = "path/to/bdi_pop_2016.tif"),   pop_raster_u5 = list("2016" = "path/to/bdi_pop_u5_2016.tif"),   path_dhs_parquet = "path/to/parquet",   table_out_path        = "output/tables",   raster_out_path       = "output/rasters",   intermediate_out_path = "output/intermediate",   survey_year = 2016,   indicators  = c("pfpr", "itn", "csb", "act", "eff_cm") )  # long-format tables at each admin level, matching the DHS output shape results$final_dataset$adm0 results$final_dataset$adm1 results$final_dataset$adm2

title: Spatial modeling (MBG)
text: Supply age-specific population rasters accurate denominators: pop_raster_u5 (child indicators), pop_raster_wra (ANC/IPTp), pop_raster_1_2 (EPI 12-23 months), age-band rasters ITN stratification. U5MR expressed per 1,000 live births; coverage indicators percentages.

title: Spatial modeling (MBG)

what: sntmethods - analytical methods for Subnational Tailoring
title: sntmethods - analytical methods for Subnational Tailoring
text: Analytical methods Sub-National Tailoring (SNT) malaria control strategies. sntmethods turns DHS/MIS survey microdata routine health facility data survey-weighted indicators, model-based geostatistical surfaces, incidence estimates sub-national program planning. analytical companion sntutils (data /O, cleaning, dictionaries, spatial validation).

title: sntmethods - analytical methods for Subnational Tailoring
text: Three workflows, dedicated guide package website: DHS survey analysis — survey-weighted, design-correct estimates 100+ indicators across 16 domains, confidence intervals admin stratification. Every family ships machine-readable data dictionary, can inspect available DHS variables building indicator. Spatial modeling (MBG) — model-based geostatistics turns cluster-level survey data continuous raster surfaces population-weighted admin estimates, via fit_mbg_indicator() (single indicator) run_mbg_pipeline() (full pipeline across 14 indicator families). Routine data: incidence & TPR — malaria incidence facility data using N0-N5 cascade, test positivity structured fallbacks, STL + Mann-Kendall trend analysis. estimators return tidy, long-format tables keyed admin level (adm0, adm1, adm2) point, ci_l, ci_u, numerator, denominator, indicator metadata — survey modeled estimates stack cleanly.

what: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
title: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
text: Computes full set (World Malaria Report) ACT treatment indicators DHS Children's Recode (KR) data. Returns survey-weighted proportions logit confidence intervals standardized long format.

title: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
code: calc_act_dhs(   dhs_kr,   dhs_kr_raw = NULL,   survey_vars = list(cluster = "v021", weight = "v005", stratum = "v022", age = "hw1",     fever = "h22", alive = "b5", act = "ml13e", test = "ml13a"),   region_var = NULL,   gps_data = NULL,   gps_vars = list(cluster = "DHSCLUST", lat = "LATNUM", lon = "LONGNUM"),   shapefile = NULL,   admin_level = NULL,   join_nearest = TRUE,   dhs_pr = NULL,   indicators = NULL,   ci_method = "logit" )

title: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
text: dhs_kr DHS Children's Recode (KR) dataset (data.frame tibble). read via dhs_read(), haven labels may standardised. Supply dhs_kr_raw accurate ACT variable detection. dhs_kr_raw Optional un-standardised KR dataset (e.g., arrow::read_parquet()) original survey-specific haven labels. provided, ACT antimalarial variables detected labels, extra variables (e.g., ml13aa, ml13da) copied analysis dataset. "two-pass" approach needed dhs_read() strips country-specific drug names. survey_vars Named list mapping DHS variable names. Required keys: cluster: Cluster/PSU ID (default: "v021") weight: Survey weight (default: "v005") stratum: Stratum variable (default: "v022") age: Child's age months (default: "hw1") fever: fever last 2 weeks (default: "h22") alive: Child alive (default: "b5") act: ACT variable(s) (default: "ml13e"; auto-detected labels) test: Test-positive filter variable (default: "ml13a") region_var Optional column name subnational grouping (e.g., "v024"). gps_data Optional DHS GE (Geographic) dataset cluster coordinates. gps_vars Named list GE variables: cluster, lat, lon. shapefile Optional sf object administrative boundaries. admin_level Character vector admin columns shapefile. join_nearest Logical; TRUE, assigns unmatched clusters nearest admin unit. Default: TRUE. dhs_pr Optional DHS Person Recode (PR) febrile RDT indicators. indicators Character vector indicator names compute. NULL (default), computes indicators act_dictionary(). ci_method Method confidence intervals. Default: "logit".

title: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
text: Named list tibbles, one per admin level: adm0 National-level estimates (always present) adm1 Admin-1 estimates (region_var shapefile used) adm2 Admin-2 estimates (shapefile adm2 used) tibble contains columns: survey_id: Survey identifier (e.g., "TG2017DHS") iso3: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., "TGO") iso2: DHS 2-letter country code (e.g., "TG") survey_type: Survey type ("DHS", "MIS", "AIS") survey_year: Survey year (integer) adm0: Country name UPPERCASE (e.g., "TOGO") adm1, adm2: Admin names UPPERCASE (subnational tabs ) type: Analysis type ("survey_weighted") geo_source: Source admin names ("survey" "gps") point: Survey-weighted proportion (0-1 scale) ci_l, ci_u: Lower upper 95\ numerator: Unweighted numerator count (has_act == 1 filtered subgroup) denominator: Unweighted denominator count (condition-filtered subgroup size) indicator: Indicator name Title Case (e.g., "Act Antimalarial") indicator_code: Short indicator code (e.g., "act_am") numerator_description: Description numerator denominator_description: Description denominator denominator_code: Short code denominator subpopulation

title: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
text: Computes 12 ACT indicators following DHS methodology. indicator measures proportion febrile U5 children receiving ACT within specific subpopulation defined care-seeking behaviour antimalarial receipt. See act_dictionary() full indicator list. function uses three internal helpers: .prepare_act_data() ACT variable detection febrile U5 filtering .classify_csb_from_h32() care-seeking behaviour classification Antimalarial composite built ml13/h37 drug series

title: Calculate ACT Treatment Indicators from DHS Data — calc_act_dhs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Two-pass approach (recommended): read standardised + raw kr <- sntmethods::dhs_read(path, file_type = "KR", ...) kr_raw <- arrow::read_parquet(parquet_path) act <- calc_act_dhs(dhs_kr = kr, dhs_kr_raw = kr_raw)  # By region act <- calc_act_dhs(dhs_kr = kr, dhs_kr_raw = kr_raw, region_var = "v024")  # With GE spatial join act <- calc_act_dhs(   dhs_kr = kr, dhs_kr_raw = kr_raw,   gps_data = ge_data,   shapefile = admin_sf,   admin_level = "adm1" )  # Subset of indicators act <- calc_act_dhs(   dhs_kr = kr, dhs_kr_raw = kr_raw,   indicators = c("ACT_ANTIMALARIAL", "ACT_PUBLIC_ANTIMALARIAL") ) } # }

title: Prepare ACT and Antimalarial Data for MBG Analysis — calc_act_mbg
text: dhs_kr DHS Children's Recode (KR) dataset. gps_data DHS GPS dataset cluster coordinates. dhs_pr Optional DHS Person Recode (PR) dataset. Required "febrile_rdt_pos" "febrile_rdt_pos_act" indicators (provides hml35). indicators Character vector indicators calculate. See .act_mbg_dictionary() full list standardized indicator codes. Legacy names "act_pub" "act_among_am" also accepted. Special indicators: "act_tested": ACT among test-positive "febrile_rdt_pos": RDT positivity (requires dhs_pr) "febrile_rdt_pos_act": ACT among RDT-positive (requires dhs_pr) Default: c("act", "act_tested"). survey_vars Named list mapping DHS variable names: cluster: Cluster ID (default: "v001") age: Child's age months (default: "hw1") fever: Fever last 2 weeks (default: "h22") alive: Child alive (default: "b5") act: ACT variable (default: "ml13e") test: Diagnostic test variable (default: "ml13a") gps_vars Named list GPS variable mapping.

text: dictionary-based indicators share data preparation pipeline: Filter febrile U5 children (via .prepare_act_data()) Classify care-seeking sectors (via .classify_csb_from_h32()) Build antimalarial composite ml13/h37 series Build malaria diagnostic flag ml1/h47 Apply per-indicator filters aggregate cluster-level counts dictionary includes three indicator families: ACT (act_*): ACT receipt among febrile U5, sector filters Antimalarial (antimal_*): Antimalarial receipt among febrile U5, sector filters Malaria diagnostic (mal_dx_*): Malaria diagnostic test (ml1/h47) among recipients, sector filters

text: Methodology: https://github.com/ahadi-analytics/sntmethods/blob/master/inst/methods/csb_dhs.yml function implements World Malaria Report methodology care-seeking behavior analysis. 5-Category Classification: public: Government health facilities (hospitals, health centers, posts) chw: Community health workers (often NGO sector DHS-8) private_formal: Private hospitals, clinics, doctors private_informal: Traditional practitioners informal sources pharmacy: Pharmacies drug shops Derived Indicators (OVERLAPPING): indicators mutually exclusive. child can counted multiple categories visited multiple source types. dhs_csb_public: Public sector care (public chw) dhs_csb_private: private sector care (private_formal private_informal pharmacy) dhs_csb_trained: Trained provider (public private_formal pharmacy) dhs_csb_any: treatment sought (public private) dhs_csb_none: treatment sought () Important: dhs_csb_any dhs_csb_none mutually exclusive. equation dhs_csb_any + dhs_csb_none = 1 always holds. However, dhs_csb_public + dhs_csb_private + dhs_csb_none may exceed 1.0 children visit public private sources.

title: Calculate Malaria Incidence from Routine Health Facility Data (N0-N5) — calc_incidence
text: Calculates malaria incidence admin-month level (e.g., district-month) using structured cascade framework (N0 N5) adjust testing gaps, reporting incompleteness, care-seeking behavior. Facility-level data aggregated admin level calculating incidence. Returns validated dataset incidence levels, quality flags, source tracking.

title: Calculate Malaria Incidence from Routine Health Facility Data (N0-N5) — calc_incidence
code: calc_incidence(   data,   levels = c("N0", "N1", "N2", "N3"),   hf_var = "hf_uid",   adm0_var = NULL,   adm1_var = "adm1",   adm2_var = "adm2",   adm3_var = NULL,   date_var = "date",   year_var = "year",   pop_var = "pop",   conf_var = "conf",   test_var = "test",   pres_var = "pres",   tpr_var = "tpr",   reprate_var = "reprate",   cs_public_var = "cs_public",   cs_private_var = "cs_private",   cs_none_var = "cs_none",   cs_none_divisor = 2,   rate_multiplier = 1000,   scale_factor = lifecycle::deprecated(),   include_flags = FALSE,   suffix = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   return_facility = FALSE )

title: Calculate Malaria Incidence from Routine Health Facility Data (N0-N5) — calc_incidence
text: data Routine health facility data facility-month level (data.frame tibble). Must contain one row per facility per month. levels Character vector specifying incidence levels calculate (default: c("N0", "N1", "N2", "N3")). Can specify subset like c("N0", "N1") include N4/N5 c("N0", "N1", "N2", "N3", "N4", "N5"). hf_var Column name health facility unique identifier (default: "hf_uid"). Set NULL data already aggregated admin level facility identifier. adm0_var Column name national/country level. NULL (default), creates single "country" value records. adm1_var Column name first administrative level/region (default: "adm1"). adm2_var Column name second administrative level/district (default: "adm2"). adm3_var Column name third administrative level/sub-district. NULL (default), adm3 column included output. date_var Column name date reporting period (default: "date"). year_var Column name year (default: "year"). Used year-level aggregation summary output. pop_var Column name population denominator (default: "pop"). Must present data. conf_var Column name number confirmed malaria cases (default: "conf"). test_var Column name number individuals tested (default: "test"). pres_var Column name number presumed cases (default: "pres"). tpr_var Column name test positivity rate (default: "tpr"). Must present data N1/N2/N3 calculations. reprate_var Column name reporting rate (default: "reprate"). Must present data N2/N3 calculations. cs_public_var Column name proportion seeking care public facilities (default: "cs_public"). Required N3. cs_private_var Column name proportion seeking care private facilities (default: "cs_private"). Required N3. cs_none_var Column name proportion seeking care (default: "cs_none"). Required N3/N4/N5. cs_none_divisor Numeric. Divisor applied cs_none N5 calculation (default: 2). N5 uses cs_none / cs_none_divisor instead cs_none, producing conservative estimate N4. rate_multiplier Denominator incidence rate calculation (default: 1000, cases per 1,000 population). scale_factor Deprecated. Use rate_multiplier instead. include_flags Logical; TRUE, includes quality flag columns output. FALSE (default), returns core incidence variables without flags. suffix Character string append incidence column names (default: NULL). provided (e.g., suffix = "u5"), output columns named n0_cases_u5, n0_incidence_u5, etc. Useful distinguishing outputs different population groups. group_by Character vector additional column names group (e.g. facility type, urban/rural). columns preserved aggregation appear output. Default NULL. return_facility Logical; TRUE, includes facility-level data output diagnostic flags. FALSE (default), returns aggregated admin-level data. Useful investigating data quality issues aggregation (e.g., n1_cases < n0_cases).

title: Calculate Malaria Incidence from Routine Health Facility Data (N0-N5) — calc_incidence
text: named list components: monthly named list tibbles admin level (N0-N5): adm0: National level monthly incidence (N0-N5) adm1: First admin level (region) monthly incidence (N0-N5) adm2: Second admin level (district) monthly incidence (N0-N5) adm3: Third admin level monthly incidence (N0-N5, adm3_var provided) annual named list tibbles admin level (N0-N5): adm0: National level annual incidence (N0-N5) adm1: First admin level (region) annual incidence (N0-N5) adm2: Second admin level (district) annual incidence (N0-N5) adm3: Third admin level annual incidence (N0-N5, adm3_var provided) facility (return_facility = TRUE) named list tibbles: monthly: Facility-month level data annual: Facility-year level data (applicable) Monthly annual tibbles contain: ID columns: adm0, adm1, adm2, adm3 (depending level) Time columns: year, month, date (monthly) year (annual) pop: Population denominator (summed) conf: Confirmed cases (summed) test, pres, tpr: Testing data (summed, N1+ calculated) reprate: Reporting rate (summed, N2+ calculated) cs_public, cs_private, cs_none: Care-seeking proportions (N3+ calculated) n0_cases, n0_incidence: N0 (crude) n1_cases, n1_incidence: N1 (testing-adjusted) n2_cases, n2_incidence: N2 (reporting-adjusted) n3_cases, n3_incidence: N3 (care-seeking-adjusted) n4_cases, n4_incidence: N4 (public + non-seekers) n5_cases, n5_incidence: N5 (conservative non-seekers)

title: Calculate Malaria Incidence from Routine Health Facility Data (N0-N5) — calc_incidence
text: incidence cascade framework: N0 (Crude Incidence): n0_cases = conf n0_incidence = (n0_cases / pop) * rate_multiplier N1 (Testing-Adjusted): n1_cases = n0_cases + (pres * tpr) n1_incidence = (n1_cases / pop) * rate_multiplier N2 (Reporting-Adjusted): n2_cases = n1_cases / reprate n2_incidence = (n2_cases / pop) * rate_multiplier N3 (Care-Seeking-Adjusted): n3_cases = n2_cases + (n2_cases * cs_private/cs_public) + (n2_cases * cs_none/cs_public) n3_incidence = (n3_cases / pop) * rate_multiplier N4 (Public + Non-Seekers Adjusted): n4_cases = n2_cases * (1 + cs_none/cs_public) n4_incidence = (n4_cases / pop) * rate_multiplier Note: Excludes private sector adjustment. Use private data already captured DHIS2 private burden negligible. N4 always N2 N3 magnitude (N2 < N4 < N3). N5 (Conservative Non-Seekers Adjusted): n5_cases = n2_cases * (1 + (cs_none/cs_none_divisor)/cs_public) n5_incidence = (n5_cases / pop) * rate_multiplier Note: Like N4 divides non-care-seeking proportion cs_none_divisor (default 2) conservative estimate. N5 always N2 N4 magnitude (N2 < N5 < N4). level builds previous, N3 representing complete estimate true community-level malaria incidence. N4 provides conservative alternative N3 excludes private sector. N5 provides even conservative estimate reducing non-seeker adjustment.

title: Calculate Malaria Incidence from Routine Health Facility Data (N0-N5) — calc_incidence
code: # Example workflow: Calculate TPR first, then incidence # library(tibble) # # # Step 1: Calculate TPR # facility_data <- tibble( #   hf_uid = c("HF001", "HF002", "HF003"), #   adm1 = rep("RegionA", 3), #   adm2 = rep("DistrictX", 3), #   date = as.Date(c("2023-01-01", "2023-02-01", "2023-01-01")), #   conf = c(10, 15, 12), #   test = c(100, 120, 80), #   pres = c(5, 8, 10) # ) # # tpr_data <- calc_tpr(facility_data) # # # Step 2: Add population, reporting rate, and care-seeking data # tpr_data$pop <- c(5000, 6000, 4500) # tpr_data$reprate <- c(0.85, 0.90, 0.80) # tpr_data$cs_public <- 0.60 # tpr_data$cs_private <- 0.25 # tpr_data$cs_none <- 0.15 # # # Step 3: Calculate incidence (all levels N0-N3) # result <- calc_incidence(tpr_data) # # # Access monthly facility-level data # result$monthly$hf # # # Access monthly district-level data # result$monthly$adm2 # # # Access annual facility-level data # result$annual$hf # # # Access annual national-level data # result$annual$adm0

title: Calculate ITN Indicators from DHS Data — calc_itn_dhs
text: dhs_hr DHS Household Records dataset (HR). dhs_pr DHS Person Records dataset (PR). survey_vars Named list mapping DHS variable names. Required keys: cluster: Cluster ID (default: "hv001") weight: Survey weight (default: "hv005") stratum: Stratum variable (default: "hv022") hhid: Household ID (default: "hhid") hhsize: Household size (default: "hv013") age: Age years (default: "hv105") sex: Sex (default: "hv104") pregnant: Pregnancy status (default: "hml18") itn_use: Slept ITN last night (default: "hml12") itn_prefix: Prefix ITN net variables (default: "hml10_") wealth: Wealth index quintile (default: "hv270") residence: Urban/rural (default: "hv025") region_var Optional column name subnational grouping (e.g., "hv024"). Auto-falls back "hv024" spatial params. gps_data Optional DHS GPS dataset cluster coordinates. gps_vars Named list GE variables: cluster, lat, lon. shapefile Optional sf object administrative boundaries. admin_level Character vector admin columns shapefile. join_nearest Logical; assign unmatched clusters nearest polygon. indicators Character vector indicator names compute. NULL (default), computes indicators itn_dictionary(). age_breaks Optional numeric vector age group boundaries (e.g., c(0, 5, 15, Inf)). Creates additional USE_ITN_AGE_* indicators. age_labels Optional character vector labels age groups (e.g., c("U5", "5_14", "OV15")). Must length = length(age_breaks) - 1. ci_method Method confidence intervals. Default: "logit".

text: Named list tibbles, one per admin level: adm0 National-level estimates (always present) adm1 Admin-1 estimates (region_var shapefile used) adm2 Admin-2 estimates (shapefile adm2 used) tibble contains columns: survey_id, iso3, iso2, survey_type, survey_year, adm0, adm1, adm2, type, geo_source, point, ci_l, ci_u, numerator, denominator, indicator, indicator_code, numerator_description, denominator_description, denominator_code.

title: Calculate ITN Indicators from DHS Data — calc_itn_dhs
text: Computes 42+ ITN indicators following methodology, organised 6 categories: ENOUGH_ITN (household sufficient nets), WITH_ITN (household ownership), ACCESS_ITN (population access), USE_ITN_CHU5 (5 use), USE_ITN_PREGNANT (pregnant women use), USE_ITN (general population use). category includes 7 subgroup splits: overall, LOW_WEALTH, HIGH_WEALTH, NON_LOW_WEALTH, NON_HIGH_WEALTH, RURAL, URBAN. age_breaks age_labels provided, additional USE_ITN_AGE_* indicators computed age group.

title: Prepare ITN Data for MBG Analysis — calc_itn_mbg
text: dhs_hr DHS Household Records dataset. dhs_pr DHS Person Records dataset. gps_data DHS GPS dataset cluster coordinates. indicators Character vector indicators calculate. Options .itn_mbg_dictionary(): "with_itn": Households least one ITN (HR) "enough_itn": Households enough ITNs every 2 people (HR) "access_itn": Population access ITN – binary indicator (PR) "use_itn": Population used ITN last night (PR) "use_itn_chu5": -5 children used ITN (PR) "use_itn_5_10": Children 5-9 years used ITN (PR) "use_itn_10_20": Adolescents 10-19 years used ITN (PR) "use_itn_20plus": Adults 20+ used ITN (PR) "use_itn_preg": Pregnant women used ITN (PR) "use_itn_if_access": access, proportion used ITN (PR) Default: indicators. survey_vars Named list mapping DHS variable names. gps_vars Named list GPS variable mapping. seed Deprecated. Previously used probabilistic access assignment. Access now calculated deterministically following standard DHS methodology.

title: Prepare ITN Data for MBG Analysis — calc_itn_mbg
text: list data.tables (one per indicator), columns: cluster_id: Cluster identifier indicator: Numerator count samplesize: Denominator count x: Longitude y: Latitude

title: Prepare ITN Data for MBG Analysis — calc_itn_mbg
text: Uses dictionary-driven approach matching indicator codes calc_itn_dhs. dictionary mirrors DHS .itn_conditions() – outcome variables, filters, data sources (HR vs PR). Methodology: https://github.com/ahadi-analytics/sntmethods/blob/master/inst/methods/itn_dhs.yml function prepares data MBG spatial modeling. Unlike survey- weighted calc_itn_dhs() function, uses simple cluster-level counts. ITN access calculated using standard DHS deterministic assignment method: Calculate potential users per household: min(ITNs * 2, household_size) Sort individuals within household ITN use (users first) Assign access first N individuals N = potential_users method guarantees use <= access individual level, since anyone used ITN prioritised access assignment.

what: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
title: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
text: Calculates malaria Test Positivity Rate (TPR) health facility-month level applies structured fallback logic derive proxy values confirmed tested data missing. Input data must health facility-month level, one observation per facility per reporting month. Returns validated TPR dataset quality flags source tracking.

title: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
code: calc_tpr(   data,   hf_var = "hf_uid",   adm0_var = NULL,   adm1_var = "adm1",   adm2_var = "adm2",   date_var = "date",   conf_var = "conf",   test_var = "test",   extreme_threshold = c(0.01, 0.99),   include_flags = FALSE,   activity_indicators = c("conf", "test"),   activity_method = 3,   nonreport_window = 6,   fallback_method = c("rolling", "adm2", "prev_year", "adm1", "adm0"),   prev_year_window = 2,   fallback_triggers = c("missing", "extreme", "low_test") )

title: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
text: data Routine health facility data facility-month level (data.frame tibble). Must contain one row per facility per month. hf_var Column name health facility unique identifier (default: "hf_uid"). adm0_var Column name national/country level. NULL (default), creates single "country" value records. adm1_var Column name first administrative level/region (default: "adm1"). adm2_var Column name second administrative level/district (default: "adm2"). date_var Column name date reporting period (default: "date"). conf_var Column name number confirmed malaria cases (default: "conf"). test_var Column name number individuals tested (default: "test"). extreme_threshold Numeric vector length 2 specifying lower upper bounds flagging extreme TPR values (default: c(0.01, 0.99)). include_flags Logical; TRUE, includes quality flag columns output. FALSE (default), returns core TPR variables without flags. activity_indicators Character vector indicator columns used determine facility activity. Default c("conf", "test"). activity_method Numeric. Classification method facility activity (1, 2, 3). Default 3 (dynamic activation/inactivation). Used flag inactive facility-months excluded proxy calculations. nonreport_window Integer. Number consecutive non-reporting periods facility considered inactive (method 3). Default 6. fallback_method Character vector specifying proxy fallback levels use order. Valid options: "rolling" (3-month rolling average facility), "adm2" (district-level), "adm1" (regional-level), "prev_year" (month previous year), "adm0" (national-level). Default c("rolling", "adm2", "prev_year", "adm1", "adm0"). Proxies applied sequentially order specified. Set NULL disable fallbacks (raw TPR ). prev_year_window Integer specifying seasonal window months previous year fallback. 0 = exact month match (default), 1 = +/-1 month window (3-month average), 2 = +/-2 months (5-month average), etc. Maximum value 6. window > 0, averages available months within window previous year, weighted test counts. fallback_triggers Character vector specifying apply proxy fallbacks. Valid options: "missing" (conf/test NA), "extreme" (TPR outside extreme_threshold), "low_test" (test < 5). Default c("missing", "extreme", "low_test"). Set NULL disable triggers (keep raw TPR values without replacement). Note: impossible values (conf > test) always included fallback along missing values. Inactive facilities always excluded.

title: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
text: list containing three elements: data: tibble TPR estimates per facility-month including: hf_uid: Health facility identifier adm0: National/country level adm1: First administrative level adm2: Second administrative level date: Standardised date (first month) year: Year extracted date month: Month extracted date conf: Confirmed cases test: Number tested tpr: Final validated proxy TPR (0-1 scale) tpr_source: Source TPR value (facility_raw, proxy_adm2, proxy_adm1, proxy_prev_year, proxy_adm0) flag_tpr_valid: TRUE raw TPR calculated flag_tpr_extreme: TRUE TPR outside extreme_threshold flag_tpr_proxy: TRUE proxy value used flag_tpr_missing: TRUE TPR assigned flag_conf_gt_test: TRUE conf > test (impossible value) flag_zero_test: TRUE test == 0 flag_low_test: TRUE test < 5 flag_missing_conf: TRUE conf NA flag_inactive: TRUE facility-month inactive

title: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
text: default fallback hierarchy proxy TPR values : Rolling 3-month average facility (+/-1 month) District (adm2) TPR month month previous year (facility-level) Regional (adm1) TPR month National (adm0) TPR month

title: Calculate Test Positivity Rate from Routine Health Facility Data — calc_tpr
text: function calculate reporting rates. need reporting rates analysis filtering, calculate separately using sntutils::calculate_reporting_metrics() join data calling calc_tpr(). Example:
code: # Calculate reporting rate at district level reprate_data <- sntutils::calculate_reporting_metrics(   data = facility_data,   vars_of_interest = "conf",   x_var = "date",   y_var = "adm2",   hf_col = "hf_uid",   key_indicators = c("conf", "test") )  # Join back to facility data (include all admin levels for proper join) data_with_reprate <- facility_data |>   dplyr::left_join(     reprate_data |> dplyr::select(adm0, adm1, adm2, date, reprate),     by = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "date")   )  # Now filter or use reprate in your analysis tpr_result <- calc_tpr(data_with_reprate) |>   dplyr::filter(reprate >= 0.8)

text: Prepares cluster-level -5 Mortality Rate (U5MR) data MBG analysis using DHS.rates::chmort() mortality calculation. follows standard DHS synthetic-cohort life-table methodology 8 age segments.

title: Prepare U5MR Data for MBG Analysis — calc_u5mr_mbg
code: calc_u5mr_mbg(   dhs_br,   gps_data,   period_years = 5,   survey_vars = list(cluster = "v001", interview_date = "v008", birth_date = "b3",     age_at_death = "b7"),   gps_vars = list(cluster = "DHSCLUST", lat = "LATNUM", lon = "LONGNUM") )

title: Prepare U5MR Data for MBG Analysis — calc_u5mr_mbg
text: dhs_br DHS Birth Recode (BR) dataset. Must contain standard DHS variables needed DHS.rates::chmort(): cluster ID (v001), interview date CMC (v008), child's date birth CMC (b3), child's age death months (b7). gps_data DHS GPS dataset cluster coordinates. period_years Number years look back mortality reference period. Default: 5 (standard DHS 5-year window). Passed DHS.rates::chmort() Period = period_years * 12. survey_vars Named list mapping DHS variable names. Keys: cluster: Cluster ID variable (default: "v001") interview_date: Date interview CMC (default: "v008") birth_date: Child's date birth CMC (default: "b3") age_at_death: Child's age death months (default: "b7") gps_vars Named list GPS variable mapping.

title: Prepare U5MR Data for MBG Analysis — calc_u5mr_mbg
text: list one data.table named "u5mr" containing: cluster_id: Cluster identifier indicator: Estimated number deaths (derived U5MR exposure) samplesize: Number births exposed 0-60 month window x: Longitude y: Latitude u5mr: U5MR per 1,000 live births Returns NULL required variables missing data insufficient.

title: Prepare U5MR Data for MBG Analysis — calc_u5mr_mbg
text: Methodology: https://github.com/ahadi-analytics/sntmethods/blob/master/inst/methods/u5mr_dhs.yml DHS.rates::chmort() computes childhood mortality rates (NNMR, PNNMR, IMR, CMR, U5MR) using standard DHS synthetic-cohort approach. called Class = "v001" (cluster ID), produces per-cluster U5MR estimates. function uses 8 age segments (0-1, 1-3, 3-6, 6-12, 12-24, 24-36, 36-48, 48-60 months) applies partial-exposure weighting period boundaries. chmort() internally computes design effect (DEFT) via survey::svydesign(), per-cluster subsets contain single PSU, function creates synthetic PSU strata columns two pseudo-PSUs per cluster. Uniform weights applied cluster-level rates unweighted – appropriate MBG, handles spatial smoothing uncertainty internally. Important: indicator samplesize columns used MBG model death proportion (indicator/samplesize). MBG pipeline automatically converts model outputs "per 1,000" units match epidemiological standards scale u5mr column.

title: Prepare U5MR Data for MBG Analysis — calc_u5mr_mbg
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ u5mr_mbg <- calc_u5mr_mbg(   dhs_br = br_data,   gps_data = gps_data ) # Access combined U5MR u5mr_mbg$u5mr } # }

text: dhs_data_path() builds robust, OS-aware path file folder inside AHADI OneDrive "Shared Library". : detects OS (Windows macOS) scans common OneDrive roots (including CloudStorage macOS) finds "Shared Libraries" folder given organisation fuzzy-matches requested library name navigates given base folder inside library optionally joins relative path function caches resolved library root R option subsequent calls fast. Use refresh = TRUE force re-detection.

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — dhs_data_path
code: dhs_data_path(   relative = NULL,   org = "Applied Health Analytics for Delivery and Innovation Inc",   library = "AHADI Information - technical",   base = "Internal docs and resources/data/dhs_data",   refresh = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — dhs_data_path
text: relative character. Relative path inside base. NULL, function returns resolved base folder path. org character. Organisation name appears OneDrive shared library naming. cleaned internally matching. Default "Applied Health Analytics Delivery Innovation Inc". library character. Display name OneDrive shared library. Example: "AHADI Information - technical". base character. Base folder inside library. Example: "Documentation per topic/data/dhs_data". refresh logical. TRUE, ignores cached location forces fresh search. verbose logical. TRUE, prints diagnostic messages path resolved.

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — dhs_data_path
text: character scalar giving absolute path. relative NULL, resolved base path. Otherwise file.path(base, relative).

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — dhs_data_path
text: detection logic follows several steps: detect OS using Sys.info()[["sysname"]] construct list candidate OneDrive root folders within root, look folders whose names suggest OneDrive "Shared Libraries" mount inside , fuzzy match requested organisation library names construct validate requested base folder Matching done "cleaned" names (lowercase, spaces punctuation). makes function resilient cosmetic differences naming "AHADI Information - technical" vs "AHADI information (technical)". function find matching organisation, library, base folder, fails cli::cli_abort() prints closest matches see.

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — dhs_data_path

title: Read a DHS recode from a hive-partitioned parquet archive — dhs_read
text: dhs_read() expects path root directory tree partitioned order, one parquet file per survey leaf:   Partition keys read literally file_type=..., country_code=..., survey_year=..., survey_id=.... Allowed file_type values PR, HR, IR, KR, GE, BR, MR, WI. country_code DHS two-letter code (e.g. TG, BU, KE).
code: path/   file_type=GE/     country_code=TG/       survey_year=2017/         survey_id=TGGE8I/           data.parquet   file_type=KR/     country_code=TG/       survey_year=2017/         survey_id=TGKR81/           data.parquet   file_type=PR/...

title: Read a DHS recode from a hive-partitioned parquet archive — dhs_read
text: use dhs_read() (therefore run_mbg_pipeline()) data, convert raw DHS recode parquet file matching leaf path. minimal recipe per file:   Repeat per recode (GE/PR/HR/KR/IR) per survey. Keep haven labels columns - dhs_read() indicator functions rely .
code: library(arrow); library(haven); library(fs)  raw <- haven::read_dta("TGKR81FL.DTA")          # preserves labels raw$file_type    <- "KR" raw$country_code <- "TG" raw$survey_year  <- 2017L raw$survey_id    <- "TGKR81" raw$survey_type  <- "DHS"                       # or "MIS"  leaf <- fs::path("path/to/parquet",                  "file_type=KR",                  "country_code=TG",                  "survey_year=2017",                  "survey_id=TGKR81") fs::dir_create(leaf) arrow::write_parquet(raw, fs::path(leaf, "data.parquet"))

title: Read a DHS recode from a hive-partitioned parquet archive — dhs_read
text: country_code survey_year (optionally survey_id) identify single survey, dhs_read() calls arrow::read_parquet() directly haven labels survey-specific variables preserved. filter spans multiple surveys, falls back arrow::open_dataset() standardises labels drops variables absent surveys. indicator work, prefer single-survey path. Defensive deduplication runs standard recode keys guarantee one row per respondent unit (DHS parquet files contain duplicate rows).

text: Generic, low-level wrapper around mbg::MbgModelRunner takes pre-built cluster-level table plus population raster subset admin-0/1/2/3 shapefiles, fits single MBG model returns comprehensive list -memory artefacts (cluster table, mean/lower/upper rasters, admin-level long-format tibbles, fitted MbgModelRunner object, id raster, aggregation tables, cell-prediction draws matrix inputs echo every parameter function received).

title: Fit a Single MBG Indicator (Generic, Pluggable Admin Levels) — fit_mbg_indicator
code: fit_mbg_indicator(   cluster_data,   indicator_name,   population_raster,   adm0_sf = NULL,   adm1_sf = NULL,   adm2_sf = NULL,   adm3_sf = NULL,   primary_level = NULL,   output_levels = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   pixel_size = 0.04166667,   n_samples = 250,   seed = 1,   cluster_cols = list(cluster_id = "cluster_id", x = "x", y = "y", indicator =     "indicator", samplesize = "samplesize"),   id_field = "shapeID",   indicator_title = NULL,   indicator_unit_scale = 100,   survey_year = NULL,   source_label = NULL,   output_dir = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   use_cache = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE,   return_draws = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Fit a Single MBG Indicator (Generic, Pluggable Admin Levels) — fit_mbg_indicator
text: cluster_data data-frame-like object (data.frame, tibble, data.table, sf x/y columns). Coerced internally via data.table::.data.table(). Must contain columns cluster_id, x, y, indicator, samplesize (supply alternative names via cluster_cols). indicator_name Character. Short slug used filenames admin-tibble columns (e.g. "pfpr_mic_2_10"). population_raster terra::SpatRaster used template raster prediction weights population- weighted aggregation. Required. adm0_sf, adm1_sf, adm2_sf, adm3_sf Optional sf polygon layers admin level. least one must supplied. default modelling level highest provided (.e. adm3 supplied, otherwise adm2, etc.). primary_level Character, one "adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3". level MBG model fitted level cell-predictions aggregated first. Defaults highest level shapefile supplied. output_levels Character vector admin levels summarise . Defaults admin levels shapefile supplied. covariates Optional named list terra::SpatRaster objects use covariates. NULL, intercept-constant raster built automatically (pure spatial smoothing). pixel_size Numeric. Informational pixel size degrees, echoed $inputs downstream reporting. actual prediction grid inherited population_raster; argument resample raster. Default 0.04166667 (\(\approx\) 5 km equator). n_samples Integer. Number posterior draws drawn fitted model. Default 250. seed Integer. RNG seed reproducibility. Default 1. cluster_cols Named list mapping canonical cluster columns user-supplied column names. Defaults list(cluster_id = "cluster_id", x = "x", y = "y", indicator   = "indicator", samplesize = "samplesize"). id_field Character. Column name shapefiles holding polygon id (default "shapeID"; falls back unique row index present). indicator_title Optional human-readable label indicator, used column headers / messaging. Defaults title-cased version indicator_name. indicator_unit_scale Numeric. Scaling factor applied computing admin-level point estimates CIs smoothed probability surface. Default 100 (.e. percentages). survey_year Optional integer survey year. Default NULL. source_label Optional character data source label ("DHS", "MIS", "routine", ...). Generic replacement older survey_type argument. Default NULL. output_dir Optional path. NULL (default), nothing written disk. supplied, rasters/, cluster_data/ final_data/ subfolders created pipeline-style files written. cache_dir Optional path cached id-raster, aggregation table(s) cell-prediction matrix. NULL output_dir supplied, defaults file.path(   output_dir, "cache"). NULL, caching performed. use_cache Logical. FALSE, caches ignored read (still written cache_dir set). Default TRUE. overwrite Logical. TRUE, ignore existing cell- prediction cache entries always refit. Default FALSE. return_draws Logical. TRUE, full n_pixel x n_samples draws matrix returned $cell_predictions$draws. Default FALSE (memory- conservative). verbose Logical. TRUE, print step--step CLI messages. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded mbg::build_aggregation_table() mbg::MbgModelRunner$new() (filtered function's formals unrecognised names silently ignored).

title: Fit a Single MBG Indicator (Generic, Pluggable Admin Levels) — fit_mbg_indicator
text: named list elements: cluster_data cleaned input cluster table (data.table). cell_predictions Named list mean, lower, upper SpatRaster objects, optionally draws (full posterior matrix) return_draws = TRUE. admin Named list long-format tibbles, one per output_levels entry, columns        indicator, indicator_title, admin_level, admin_id, admin_name,       mean, lower, upper, population, country_iso3, country_iso2,       dhs_code, survey_year, source_label. country_iso3 / country_iso2 / dhs_code derived reverse-geocoding median cluster coordinate adm0_sf (rnaturalearth fallback) resolved via countrycode. fall back NA lookup fails. model_runner fitted MbgModelRunner object, NULL loaded cache. id_raster pixel-id raster used mbg::build_aggregation_table(). aggregation_tables Named list aggregation tables (one per output level). saved_files Named list paths written disk (empty output_dir NULL). cache_files Named list paths cache artefacts (empty cache_dir NULL). inputs Named list echoing every input parameter reproducibility.

title: Fit a Single MBG Indicator (Generic, Pluggable Admin Levels) — fit_mbg_indicator
text: function intentionally generic tied DHS. country (country_iso3 / country_iso2 / dhs_code) always derived automatically median cluster coordinate via adm0_sf (supplied) rnaturalearth::ne_countries() fallback, resolved countrycode. optional survey_year source_label arguments pure annotations: supplied echoed onto admin tibble used build pipeline-style filenames; NULL dropped filenames written NA admin tibble. output_dir NULL (default) nothing written disk – everything returned memory result list. Set output_dir directory path also write rasters, cluster file, long-format admin file (qs2 + xlsx) data dictionary, mirroring conventions run_mbg_pipeline(). cache_dir non-NULL (auto-derived output_dir), three artefacts cached: id raster (.tif), per-level aggregation table(s) (.parquet), cell-prediction draws matrix (.qs2). subsequent calls key cell-prediction matrix reloaded (expensive) INLA model fit skipped, allowing user re-summarise additional admin levels without refitting.

title: Fit a Single MBG Indicator (Generic, Pluggable Admin Levels) — fit_mbg_indicator
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Minimal in-memory call (no disk writes) fit <- fit_mbg_indicator(   cluster_data      = pfpr_dt,   indicator_name    = "pfpr_mic_2_10",   population_raster = pop_rast,   adm1_sf           = adm1, adm2_sf = adm2 ) fit$cell_predictions$mean fit$admin$adm2  # Pipeline-style call with disk + cache fit2 <- fit_mbg_indicator(   cluster_data      = pfpr_dt,   indicator_name    = "pfpr_mic_2_10",   population_raster = pop_rast,   adm1_sf = adm1, adm2_sf = adm2, adm3_sf = adm3,   primary_level     = "adm3",   output_levels     = c("adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   survey_year       = 2017,   source_label      = "MIS",   output_dir        = "outputs/mbg_fit" ) # country_iso3 / country_iso2 / dhs_code are derived from cluster coords. } # }

title: List DHS variables and their haven labels by name pattern — list_dhs_var_labels
text: data data frame (typically DHS recode KR file read dhs_read()). Columns may plain vectors haven-labelled vectors; name label attribute inspected. pattern Character scalar. Either prefix (default) regex (regex = TRUE). Must non-empty. regex Logical. FALSE (default), pattern treated prefix matched regex paste0("^", pattern, "[-z0-9]+$"). TRUE, pattern used full regex. only_observed Logical. TRUE, drop variables non-NA values, - numeric 0/1 columns - observed 1. Default FALSE. duplicate_label Logical. TRUE (default), add duplicate_label logical column flagging variables whose label shared least one matched variable.

title: List DHS variables and their haven labels by name pattern — list_dhs_var_labels
text: tibble columns: variable: matched column name. label: haven label attribute (NA_character_ absent). n_nonmissing: number non-NA values column. n_ones: number values equal 1 (NA non-numeric columns). duplicate_label (duplicate_label = TRUE): logical, TRUE another matched variable non-NA label. Variables returned order appear data. variables match, empty tibble columns returned cli info message emitted.

title: List DHS variables and their haven labels by name pattern — list_dhs_var_labels
text: Two matching modes supported: Prefix mode (default, regex = FALSE): helper matches columns whose names start pattern followed one lowercase letters digits. example, pattern = "h32" match h32a, h32x, h32a1, h32_recoded h32. Regex mode (regex = TRUE): pattern used directly regular expression. example, pattern = "^ml13[-z]$" match ITN-source variables ml13a..ml13h. Optionally, helper can: Mark variables whose haven label shared another variable inventory (duplicate_label = TRUE). situation motivates routing custom_csb_indicator spec variable name rather label (e.g. h32e h32n surveys carry label "Fever/cough: comm.health wrkr"). Drop variables observed 1 (non-NA values non-binary columns) via only_observed = TRUE. keeps inventory focused slots actually populated survey. function never modifies data; reads column names label attribute (set haven::read_dta() sntmethods::dhs_read()).

title: List DHS variables and their haven labels by name pattern — list_dhs_var_labels
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   kr_data <- sntmethods::dhs_read(     path        = path_dhs_parquet,     file_type   = "KR",     survey_type = "DHS"   )    # All h32* treatment-seeking source columns   list_dhs_var_labels(kr_data, "h32")    # Only h32 columns with at least one observed `1`, flagging duplicates   list_dhs_var_labels(kr_data, "h32", only_observed = TRUE)    # ITN source columns ml13a..ml13h via regex   list_dhs_var_labels(kr_data, "^ml13[a-z]$", regex = TRUE) } # }

title: Prepare Single CSB Indicator for MBG — prep_csb_mbg
text: dhs_kr DHS Children's Recode (KR) dataset. gps_data DHS GPS dataset cluster coordinates. indicator Single indicator name. Default: "public". csb_priority_method Character, one "" (default), "first", "public", "private". Controls overlapping care-seeking records resolved individual assigned one sector: "": Keep methodology; overlaps allowed (csb_public csb_private can 1 child). "first": Take first recurring h32 source visited per child (alphabetical h32 order: h32a, h32b, ..., h32x). Mutually exclusive. "public": child sought public/CHW care, classify public; otherwise private private; otherwise none. "private": child sought private care, classify private; otherwise public public; otherwise none. non-"" values, csb_public + csb_private + csb_none sums 100%. survey_vars Named list mapping DHS variable names. gps_vars Named list GPS variable mapping.

title: Prepare Single CSB Indicator for MBG — prep_csb_mbg
text: data.table columns: cluster_id, indicator, samplesize, x, y

code: run_mbg_pipeline(   country_iso3,   adm0_sf,   adm1_sf,   adm2_sf,   adm3_sf = NULL,   pop_raster,   path_dhs_parquet,   table_out_path,   raster_out_path,   intermediate_out_path,   pop_raster_u5 = NULL,   pop_raster_1_2 = NULL,   pop_raster_5_10 = NULL,   pop_raster_10_20 = NULL,   pop_raster_20plus = NULL,   pop_raster_wra = NULL,   country_iso2 = NULL,   survey_year = NULL,   min_year = "2005",   survey_type = NULL,   indicators = c("pfpr", "itn", "irs", "anc", "csb", "anemia", "iptp", "epi", "fever",     "antimalarial"),   aggregation_level = c("adm2", "adm3"),   run_mbg = TRUE,   save_rasters = TRUE,   save_cluster_points = TRUE,   cluster_points_out_path = NULL,   cache = TRUE,   csb_priority_method = c("all", "first", "public", "private"),   custom_csb_indicator = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   debug = FALSE )

title: Run MBG Indicator Pipeline — run_mbg_pipeline
text: country_iso3 Three-letter ISO country code (e.g., "bdi"). adm0_sf sf object country boundary. adm1_sf sf object ADM1 boundaries. adm2_sf sf object ADM2 boundaries. adm3_sf sf object ADM3 boundaries (optional). Required aggregation_level = "adm3". contain columns adm3 names parent adm2/adm1/adm0 linkages. pop_raster Total population raster(s). Can : Named list years names file paths values: list("2019" = "path//pop_2019.tif", "2020" = "path//pop_2020.tif") Single file path (used years) Already-loaded SpatRaster object (used years) path_dhs_parquet Path DHS parquet archive. table_out_path Output directory tables (CSV, XLSX). raster_out_path Output directory prediction rasters. intermediate_out_path Output directory cached intermediate outputs (aggregation tables, ID rasters). pop_raster_u5 -5 population raster(s) (optional). format pop_raster. Used indicators targeting children 0-59 months (PfPR, fever, CSB, ACT, antimalarial, anemia, U5MR, SMC, ITN use U5). NULL, falls back pop_raster. pop_raster_1_2 Population raster children 12-23 months (optional). format pop_raster. Used EPI (immunization) indicators. Falls back pop_raster_u5, pop_raster NULL. pop_raster_5_10 Population raster ages 5-10 (optional). format pop_raster. Used use_itn_5_10. Falls back pop_raster NULL. pop_raster_10_20 Population raster ages 10-20 (optional). format pop_raster. Used use_itn_10_20. Falls back pop_raster NULL. pop_raster_20plus Population raster ages 20+ (optional). format pop_raster. Used use_itn_20plus. Falls back pop_raster NULL. pop_raster_wra Women reproductive age (15-49) population raster (optional). format pop_raster. Used ANC, IPTp, ITN use among pregnant women. Falls back pop_raster NULL. country_iso2 Two-letter DHS country code (e.g., "BU"). NULL (default), derived automatically country_iso3 using countrycode package. survey_year Survey year(s) process. Can : NULL: Process available surveys GPS data Single integer: Process year (e.g., 2016) Integer vector: Process specific years (e.g., c(2012, 2016)) min_year Minimum survey year include. Surveys year excluded. Useful filtering older surveys may lack key indicators. Default: NULL (minimum). survey_type Survey type(s) process. Can : NULL (default): Auto-detect available survey types (DHS, MIS, etc.) Single string: Process type (e.g., "DHS") Character vector: Process specific types (e.g., c("DHS", "MIS")) indicators Character vector indicator categories process: "pfpr": Parasite prevalence "itn": ITN ownership/access/use "irs": IRS coverage "anc": ANC attendance "csb": Care-seeking behavior "act": ACT treatment (case management) "anemia": Anemia prevalence "iptp": IPTp doses "epi": EPI vaccination "u5mr": -5 mortality "smc": SMC receipt "fever": Fever prevalence (U5) "antimalarial": antimalarial treatment (febrile U5) "wealth": Wealth quintile distribution (proportion poorest/richest) "eff_cm": Effective coverage case management (derived; auto-adds "csb" "act" dependencies) aggregation_level Primary aggregation level MBG outputs. One : "adm2": Aggregate ADM2 level (default) "adm3": Aggregate ADM3 level (requires adm3_sf) run_mbg Logical. TRUE, runs MBG models. Default: TRUE. save_rasters Logical. TRUE, saves output rasters. Default: TRUE. save_cluster_points Logical. TRUE (default), saves coordinate-level (cluster-point) datasets produced calc_*_mbg() disk addition aggregated admin-level outputs. Set FALSE skip saving cluster-point files. cluster_points_out_path Output directory cluster-point (coordinate-level) datasets. NULL (default), falls back table_out_path. Use keep per-cluster point files separate location combined admin-level outputs. cache Logical. TRUE (default), reuses cached intermediate outputs (aggregation tables, ID rasters) available. Set FALSE force regeneration intermediate outputs. csb_priority_method Character, one "" (default), "first", "public", "private". Controls overlapping care-seeking records resolved CSB (wealth-stratified CSB) indicators individual assigned one sector: "": Keep methodology; overlaps allowed (csb_public csb_private can 1 child). "first": Take first recurring h32 source visited per child (alphabetical h32 order: h32a, h32b, ..., h32x). Mutually exclusive. "public": Public-sector priority - public/CHW care, classify public; otherwise private private; otherwise none. "private": Private-sector priority - private care, classify private; otherwise public public; otherwise none. non-"" values, csb_public + csb_private + csb_none sums 100%. custom_csb_indicator Optional named list defining user-specified, mutually-exclusive care-seeking partition fitted addition built-CSB family. supplied, three derived indicators produced: <name>_dhis (sought care user-listed DHIS source), <name>_nondhis (sought care user-listed non-DHIS source DHIS source), <name>_untreat (seek care user-listed source). list must four character fields: name: short alphanumeric prefix used build three derived indicator codes (e.g. "foo" -> foo_dhis, foo_nondhis, foo_untreat). dhis_locs: Character vector h32 entries map DHIS bucket. entry may either h32 variable name (e.g. "h32a") haven label string (case-insensitive). two styles can mixed vector; variable-name matches take precedence label matches h32 column referenced ways. nondhis_locs: Character vector non-DHIS bucket (dual-style semantics dhis_locs). untreat_locs: Character vector untreated bucket (dual-style semantics dhis_locs). custom_csb_indicator supplied, partition activated automatically: name added indicators unless (one three derived codes) already listed. custom triple always mutually exclusive child level (priority dhis > nondhis > untreat); admin-level estimates rescaled sum 100% per admin unit. Default: NULL (disabled). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints detailed progress. Default: TRUE. debug Logical. TRUE, prints additional diagnostic messages troubleshooting. Default: FALSE.

title: Re-exports from sntutils — sntutils_reexports
text: data data frame containing health facility data. vars_of_interest Character vector variable names assess reporting (used numerator). x_var Character. Name primary grouping variable (e.g., time period). y_var Character. Optional. Name second grouping variable (e.g., district). hf_col Character. Optional (defaults NULL). Name column containing unique health facility IDs. provided, enables facility-level analysis filtering inactive facilities (key_indicators specified). Can used without y_var. Required weighting = TRUE. key_indicators Optional. Character vector indicators used define facility activity scenario 1. Defaults c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"). method Character numeric. Classification method facility activity status. Can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3"). Defaults 3. See classify_facility_activity details. nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). require_all Logical. TRUE multiple vars_of_interest provided, calculates proportion facilities reporting variables (complete data). FALSE (default), calculates per-variable reporting rates. applies facility-level analysis (hf_col provided). weighting Logical. Whether use weighted reporting rates. TRUE, facilities weighted typical size, giving importance larger facilities overall reporting rate calculation. provides volume-adjusted measure data completeness. Default FALSE. weight_var Character. Name variable use proxy facility size (e.g., "allout" total outpatients, "test" tests done). count variable reflects facility activity/size. NULL weighting TRUE, auto-select allout, test, conf (order). weight_window Integer. Number periods rolling window calculate typical facility size. facility's weight based average size past weight_window periods. Larger windows provide stable weights may miss recent changes. Default 12. exclude_current_x Logical. Whether exclude current period calculating weights. TRUE, prevents current reporting influencing weight (avoids circularity). Default TRUE. cold_start Character. Method handling facilities insufficient history (< weight_window periods). Options: "median_within_y" (default): Uses median size facilities within y_var group (e.g., district) "median_global": Uses median size across facilities date_col Character. Column storing observation dates. Defaults "date". binary_classification Logical. TRUE, collapses categories "Active" vs "Inactive". Defaults FALSE. column Name numeric column evaluate. record_id Unique record identifier column. admin_level Character vector administrative level columns parallel grouping, ordered higher lower resolution. Defaults c("adm1", "adm2"). spatial_level Character string specifying finest spatial unit analysis (e.g., "hf_uid" facility-level). specified, admin_level defines grouping boundaries spatial_level defines unit analysis. prevents excessive grouping maintaining spatial granularity. Default hf_uid. date Date column (Date, POSIXt, parseable character string). Year, month, yearmon automatically derived column. time_mode Pooling strategy: "across_time", "within_year", "seasonal". Seasonal mode groups month across years (e.g., Januaries together), useful detecting values unusual specific month regardless year. value_type Indicator type: "count" "rate". Counts floor lower bounds 0. strictness Strictness preset: "lenient", "balanced", "strict", "advanced". Presets map method multipliers. "advanced", manual multipliers ignored. methods Character vector specifying outlier detection methods use: "iqr" (Interquartile Range), "median" (Median Absolute Deviation), "mean" (Mean +/- SD), /"consensus". Default c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"). consensus, least two methods must selected. sd_multiplier Width (SD units) mean method (used strictness = "advanced"). mad_constant Constant passed stats::mad() advanced mode (default 1.4826). mad_multiplier Width multiplier MAD method (advanced mode). iqr_multiplier Tukey fence multiplier IQR method (advanced mode). consensus_rule Number methods must agree (1, 2, 3) consensus flag call outlier. Default 2. output_profile Controls amount detail returned: "lean" (minimal columns: id, admin, date, value, consensus flag, reason), "standard" (lean + per-method flags + bounds + seasonality mode), "audit" (columns full reproducibility). Default "standard". min_n Minimum observations required active comparison bucket flagging attempted (applies seasonal bucket fallback). reporting_rate_col Optional column reporting completeness [0, 1]. reporting_rate_min Minimum acceptable reporting rate. Rows threshold receive reason = "low_reporting" flagged. key_indicators_hf Optional character vector indicator names used determine facility activeness. supplied, function uses fast path filter inactive facility-months. facility-month considered active specified key indicators non-NA values. Inactive facility-months excluded outlier detection. NULL (default), activeness filtering skipped. Typical indicators include "allout", "test", "conf". adjustment prevents false positives caused facilities start stop reporting mid-period. classify_outbreaks Logical. TRUE (default), applies outbreak classification distinguish isolated outliers sustained outbreak patterns. Consecutive outliers meeting outbreak criteria reclassified "outlier" "outbreak". particularly useful epidemiological surveillance identify disease outbreak patterns. Set FALSE disable outbreak classification. outbreak_min_run Integer. Minimum number consecutive outliers required classify outbreak (default 2). Must >= 2. outbreak_prop_tolerance Numeric. Proportional tolerance outbreak consistency (default 0.9). Values within tolerance run median considered consistent. Range: (0, 1). outbreak_max_gap Integer. Maximum allowed gap (non-outlier months) outliers can still considered part outbreak (default 1). example, outbreak_max_gap = 12, pattern "outlier-normal-outlier-outlier" classified one outbreak length 3, rather separate incidents. Set 0 strict consecutive-outbreaks. Useful real-world data reporting gaps. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informative summary showing methods applied, pooling strategy, strictness settings, guardrails, consensus rule. Default FALSE. return_summary Logical. TRUE, returns summary tibble instead filtered data. Default FALSE

title: sntmethods 0.8.0
text: calc_*_dhs() functions produce survey-weighted, design-correct estimates across 16 indicator domains (ITN, IRS, PfPR, ACT, antimalarials, fever care, ANC, IPTp, EPI, SMC, anemia, U5MR, case management, CSB, wealth). return long-format list(adm0, adm1, ...) tables confidence intervals, design effects admin-level stratification. Every indicator family ships machine-readable data dictionary (act_dictionary(), itn_dictionary(), …) plus unified dhs_dictionary(). dhs_read() reads single-survey parquet files directly preserve value labels survey-specific variables. ACT handled drug class across multiple variables (combination therapy ; monotherapies excluded). ITN use_if_access (use among access) calculated default standard age groups (u5, 5_14, ov15).

title: sntmethods 0.8.0
text: run_mbg_pipeline() orchestrates full model-based geostatistics workflow: survey discovery, cluster-data preparation, INLA/MBG fitting, raster prediction, population-weighted aggregation admin-2/3 roll-admin-1 admin-0. calc_*_mbg() / prep_*_mbg() prepare cluster-level data; fit_mbg_indicator() fits single indicator; aggregate_raster_to_admin() performs zonal statistics. Mapping helpers: generate_all_maps(), generate_indicator_map(), plot_mbg_clusters(). U5MR correctly expressed per 1,000 live births; intervention-coverage indicators percentages.

title: sntmethods 0.8.0
text: calc_tpr() structured fallback logic validate_tpr_proxies(). calc_incidence() implementing N0–N5 incidence cascade, create_incidence() check_incidence(). calculate_reporting_metrics(), classify_facility_activity(), get_active_facilities() detect_outliers() reporting-rate data-quality analytics. run_grouped_stl_trend() STL decomposition Mann-Kendall trend tests.
